titleStmt.title: Letter from D. H. Kennedy to Robert Chalmers, 23 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: D. H. Kennedy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from D. H. Kennedy to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) enclosing a letter which was sent to the Commissioner of Police. The enclosed letter mentions disturbances in Dublin including the throwing of objects and the gathering of intimidating crowds, which Kennedy requests the Commissioner to put a stop to.Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was Under-Secretary for Ireland. After the Easter Rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as Under-Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Lord Wimborne, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. He was admitted to the Irish Privy Council the same year. He then returned to the Treasury and served as Joint Permanent Secretary to the Treasury from 1916 to 1919.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8820/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
16 Mary St. Dublin, 23.5.1916 Sir R. Chalmers Dublin Castle Sir, Enclosed is a copy of letter forwarded to the Chief
Commissioner of Police Your obedient Servant D. Kennedy I Ack. II Chief Commissioner Fw report 24/5/16
16 Mary St Dublin 23.5.16 Esq Chief Commissioner of Police Sir As I was passing along Jervis St last evening
at the corner of upper Abbey St and Jervis St I was
struck with a stone or brick on the shin bone and another
struck me on the side of the head. I will not say either
was fired directly at me as the firing was between
two sets of youths none of them over 18 years of age
It seems a deplorable state of affairs that since
the Larkinite Strike this place has been left open
to the anarchy element of this quarter. I would suggest to the authorities to get the corporation
to have the place walled up (God knows it ought to be
about time) I also wish to draw your attention to the rowdy conduct carried
on in Mary and Henry Streets night after night. Girls and boys
of mature age pulling and dragging one another along the streets
obscene and indecent language. Soliciting crowds gathering
though proclamations say they should not do so. Putting
their dirty backs against door fronts newly painted
and when asked to move over fire impertinent answer s Etc. Etc.
Mr Quinn the assistant Commissioner can tell you a co operation
of the traders has been carried on for some time against the conduct
instead of getting better it seems to be worse I'm asking you will give the matter your serious consideration ffly yours D.H. Kennedy The Prime Minister
R. Chalmers
Mr Redmond
& their P.
for the
division
has been
forwards copy
of letter
DHK
